have h_main : Injective (chineseMap I) := by
    refine' fun x y h => _
    -- Assume the images of x and y under chineseMap I are equal.
    have h₁ : chineseMap I x = chineseMap I y := h
    -- Use the fact that the quotient map is surjective to find representatives for x and y.
    have h₂ : ∃ (r : R), x = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r := by
      -- Since x is in R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i, there exists some r in R such that x = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r.
      have h₃ : ∃ (r : R), x = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r := by
        classical
        -- Use the surjectivity of the quotient map.
        have h₄ : Function.Surjective (Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i)) := by
          apply Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
        -- Obtain the required r using the surjectivity.
        obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := h₄ x
        exact ⟨r, hr⟩
      exact h₃
    have h₃ : ∃ (s : R), y = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) s := by
      -- Similarly, find a representative for y.
      have h₄ : ∃ (s : R), y = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) s := by
        classical
        have h₅ : Function.Surjective (Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i)) := by
          apply Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
        obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h₅ y
        exact ⟨s, hs⟩
      exact h₄
    -- Obtain the representatives r and s for x and y, respectively.
    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := h₂
    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h₃
    -- Rewrite x and y in terms of their representatives.
    rw [hr] at h₁
    rw [hs] at h₁
    -- Use the property of chineseMap to relate the images of r and s.
    have h₄ : chineseMap I (Quotient.mk _ r) = chineseMap I (Quotient.mk _ s) := h₁
    have h₅ : (fun i : ι => Quotient.mk (I i) r) = (fun i : ι => Quotient.mk (I i) s) := by
      -- Since the images are equal, the corresponding components are equal for each i.

        apply chineseMap_mk
      have h₅₂ : chineseMap I (Quotient.mk _ s) = (fun i : ι => Quotient.mk (I i) s) := by
        apply chineseMap_mk
      rw [h₅₁] at h₄
      rw [h₅₂] at h₄
      exact h₄
    -- Deduce that r - s is in the intersection of all I i.
    have h₆ : ∀ (i : ι), Quotient.mk (I i) r = Quotient.mk (I i) s := by
      intro i
      have h₆₁ := congr_fun h₅ i
      exact h₆₁
    have h₇ : ∀ (i : ι), r - s ∈ I i := by
      intro i
      have h₇₁ : Quotient.mk (I i) r = Quotient.mk (I i) s := h₆ i
      have h₇₂ : Quotient.mk (I i) (r - s) = 0 := by
        calc
          Quotient.mk (I i) (r - s) = Quotient.mk (I i) r - Quotient.mk (I i) s := by

          _ = 0 := by rw [h₇₁]; simp
      have h₇₃ : r - s ∈ I i := by

      simpa [Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem] using h₈
    have h₁₀ : Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) s := by

      -- This follows from the fact that ChineseMap I (x - y) i = 0.
      have h₇ : ChineseMap I (x - y) i = 0 := h₅ i
      -- Since ChineseMap I (x - y) i = 0, we have (x - y) ∈ Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) ⁻¹' (0 : R ⧸ I i).
      have h₈ : ChineseMap I (x - y) i = Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) (x - y) := by
        -- This is by the definition of ChineseMap.
        simp [ChineseMap, Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply]
      rw [h₈] at h₇
      -- We have Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) (x - y) = 0, so (x - y) ∈ Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) ⁻¹' (0 : R ⧸ I i).
      simpa using h₇
    -- We have for all i : ι, (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) ⁻¹' (0 : R ⧸ I i).
    -- This means that for all i : ι, (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ I i.
    have h₇ : ∀ (i : ι), (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)) := by
      intro i
      -- We need to show that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
      -- This follows from the fact that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) ⁻¹' (0 : R ⧸ I i).
      have h₈ : (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) ⁻¹' (0 : R ⧸ I i) := h₆ i
      -- Since Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) is a ring homomorphism, it respects the ideal.
      have h₉ : Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) ⁻¹' (0 : R ⧸ I i) = (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)) := by
        -- We need to show that Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) ⁻¹' (0 : R ⧸ I i) = (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
        -- This follows from the fact that Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) is a ring homomorphism.
        ext z
        -- We need to show that Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) z = 0 ↔ z ∈ (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
        simp [Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem]
        <;>
        simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_mk_iff_sub_mem]
      -- Since Ideal.Quotient.mk (I i) ⁻¹' (0 : R ⧸ I i) = (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)), we have (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
      rw [h₉] at h₈
      exact h₈
    -- We have for all i : ι, (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
    -- This means that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ ⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
    have h₈ : (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ (⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i))) := by
      -- We need to show that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ ⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
      -- This follows from the fact that for all i : ι, (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
      exact mimpi h₇
    -- We have (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ ⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
    -- This means that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = 0.
    have h₉ : (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = 0 := by
      -- We need to show that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = 0.
      -- This follows from the fact that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ ⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
      have h₁₀ : (⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i))) = {0} := by
        -- We need to show that (⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i))) = {0}.
        -- This follows from the fact that the intersection of the ideals is {0}.
        apply Set.ext
        intro z
        -- We need to show that z ∈ ⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)) ↔ z = 0.
        simp [Set.mem_iInf, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
        <;>
        (try aesop)
        <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₁₁ : z ∈ (⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i))) := by
              -- We need to show that z ∈ ⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
              -- This follows from the fact that z = 0.
              aesop
            aesop
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₁₁ : z ∉ (⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i))) := by
              -- We need to show that z ∉ ⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i)).
              -- This follows from the fact that z ≠ 0.
              aesop
            aesop
          })
      -- Since (⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i))) = {0}, we have (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ {0}.
      have h₁₁ : (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ (⨅ i, (I i : Set (R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i))) := h₈
      rw [h₁₀] at h₁₁
      -- Since (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) ∈ {0}, we have (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = 0.
      simpa using h₁₁
    -- We have (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = 0.
    -- This means that x = y.
    have h₁₀ : (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = 0 := h₉
    -- We need to show that x = y.
    -- This follows from the fact that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = 0.
    have h₁₁ : x = y := by
      -- We need to show that x = y.
      -- This follows from the fact that (x - y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = 0.
      simpa [sub_eq_zero] using h₁₀
    -- We have x = y.
    exact h₁₁
  -- We have x = y.
  exact h₃

rw [map_injective]
  -- We use the fact that if the composition of maps is injective, then the individual maps are injective.
  -- Specifically, if the composition of the quotient map and the chineseMap is injective, then the chineseMap is injective.
  -- We need to show that if the image of two elements under the chineseMap are equal, then the two elements are equal.
  refine' fun x y h => _
  -- Suppose the images of x and y under the chineseMap are equal. We need to show that x = y.
  -- We can use the fact that the images of x and y under the quotient map are distinct in the quotient ring.
  -- Since the quotient map is injective, the elements x and y must be equal in the original ring.
  have h₁ : x = y := by
    -- Use the fact that the images of x and y under the chineseMap are equal to deduce that x = y.
    -- Specifically, if the images of x and y under the chineseMap are equal, then for every i, the images of x and y under the quotient map to R / I i are equal.
    -- This implies that x - y is in the kernel of the quotient map to R / I i, which is I i.
    -- Since this holds for every i, x - y is in the intersection of all I i, which is the ideal we are quotienting by.
    -- Therefore, x and y are in the same equivalence class in R / (intersection of all I i).
    -- But since the ChineseMap is injective, we must have x = y.
    simp_all [chineseMap_mk]
    <;> rfl
  -- Conclude that x = y.
  exact h₁

have h₁ : Function.Injective (chineseMap I) := by
    intro x y h
    have h₂ : (chineseMap I x : Π i, R ⧸ I i) = (chineseMap I y : Π i, R ⧸ I i) := by
      exact h
    have h₃ : ∀ i : ι, (chineseMap I x : Π i, R ⧸ I i) i = (chineseMap I y : Π i, R ⧸ I i) i := by
      intro i
      exact congr_fun h₂ i
    have h₄ : ∀ i : ι, (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i = (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i := by
      intro i
      have h₅ : (chineseMap I x : Π i, R ⧸ I i) i = Quotient.mk (I i) ((x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i).val) := by
        simp [chineseMap_mk', Quotient.mk_eq_mk]
        <;>
        simp_all [Quotient.lift_apply]
        <;>
        aesop
      have h₆ : (chineseMap I y : Π i, R ⧸ I i) i = Quotient.mk (I i) ((y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i).val) := by
        simp [chineseMap_mk', Quotient.mk_eq_mk]
        <;>
        simp_all [Quotient.lift_apply]
        <;>
        aesop
      have h₇ : Quotient.mk (I i) ((x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i).val) = Quotient.mk (I i) ((y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i).val) := by
        calc
          Quotient.mk (I i) ((x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i).val) = (chineseMap I x : Π i, R ⧸ I i) i := by rw [h₅]
          _ = (chineseMap I y : Π i, R ⧸ I i) i := by rw [h₃ i]
          _ = Quotient.mk (I i) ((y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i).val) := by rw [h₆]
      have h₈ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i = (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i := by
        have h₉ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ ⨅ i, I i := by
          apply Finset.mem_iInf.mpr
          intro i
          exact I i.zero_mem
        have h₁₀ : (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ ⨅ i, I i := by
          apply Finset.mem_iInf.mpr
          intro i
          exact I i.zero_mem
        have h₁₁ : Quotient.mk (I i) ((x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i) = Quotient.mk (I i) ((y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i) := by
          have h₁₂ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := by
            have h₁₃ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ ⨅ i, I i := h₉
            have h₁₄ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := by
              simp only [Ideal.mem_iInf] at h₁₃
              exact h₁₃ i
            exact h₁₄
          have h₁₅ : (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := by
            have h₁₆ : (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ ⨅ i, I i := h₁₀
            have h₁₇ : (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := by
              simp only [Ideal.mem_iInf] at h₁₆
              exact h₁₆ i
            exact h₁₇
          have h₁₈ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := by
            have h₁₉ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := by
              -- Use the fact that the difference of two elements in an ideal is in the ideal
              have h₂₀ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := h₁₂
              have h₂₁ : (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := h₁₅
              have h₂₂ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i ∈ I i := by
                apply I i.sub_mem
                <;> assumption
              exact h₂₂
            exact h₁₉
          have h₂₃ : Quotient.mk (I i) ((x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i) = Quotient.mk (I i) ((y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i) := by
            apply Quotient.mk_eq_mk.mpr
            simpa [sub_eq_zero] using h₁₈
          exact h₂₃
        simpa [Quotient.mk_eq_mk] using h₁₁
      exact h₈
    have h₅ : x = y := by
      -- Since all components are equal, the elements are equal
      have h₆ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) := by
        -- Use the fact that the components are equal to conclude the elements are equal
        exact Quotient.ext (by
          -- Use the fact that the components are equal to conclude the elements are equal
          have h₇ : ∀ i : ι, (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i = (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i := h₄
          have h₈ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) = (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) := by
            -- Use the fact that the components are equal to conclude the elements are equal
            apply Subtype.ext
            intro i
            have h₉ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i = (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i) i := h₄ i
            exact h₉
          exact h₈)
      exact h₆
    exact h₅
  exact h₁

rw [Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective]
  <;>
    (try simp_all [Submodule.coe_injective, Function.Injective]) <;>
    (try exact inferInstance) <;>
    (try

rw [Function.Injective]
  intro x y h
  have h₁ : x = y := by
    -- Use the fact that the product of the quotient maps is injective to conclude that x = y
    have h₂ : (chineseMap I x : Π i : ι, R ⧸ I i) = chineseMap I y := h
    have h₃ : ∀ (i : ι), (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ≡ (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val [ZMOD ⨅ i : ι, I i] := by
      simp_all [Quotient.eq]
    have h₄ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ≡ (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val [ZMOD ⨅ i : ι, I i] := by
      simp_all [Quotient.eq]
    have h₅ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ∈ ⨅ i : ι, I i := by
      simpa [Int.ModEq, emod_eq_emod_iff_emod_sub_eq_zero] using h₄
    have h₆ : x = y := by
      -- Use the fact that the product of the quotient maps is injective to conclude that x = y
      apply eq_of_sub_eq_zero
      have h₇ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val = 0 := by
        -- Show that the difference is in the intersection of ideals
        have h₈ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ∈ ⨅ i : ι, I i := h₅
        have h₉ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val = 0 := by
          -- Use the injectivity of the product of the quotient maps
          apply eq_of_sub_eq_zero
          have h₁₀ : (chineseMap I x : Π i : ι, R ⧸ I i) = chineseMap I y := h₂
          have h₁₁ : ∀ i : ι, (chineseMap I x i : R ⧸ I i) = chineseMap I y i := by
            intro i
            rw [h₁₀]
          intro i
          have h₁₂ : (chineseMap I x i : R ⧸ I i) = chineseMap I y i := h₁₁ i
          have h₁₃ : (chineseMap I x i : R ⧸ I i) = Quotient.mk (I i) ((x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val) := by
            simp [chineseMap_mk']
          have h₁₄ : (chineseMap I y i : R ⧸ I i) = Quotient.mk (I i) ((y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val) := by
            simp [chineseMap_mk']
          rw [h₁₃, h₁₄] at h₁₂
          have h₁₅ : Quotient.mk (I i) ((x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val) = Quotient.mk (I i) ((y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val) := h₁₂
          have h₁₆ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ≡ (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val [ZMOD I i] := by
            simpa [Quotient.eq] using h₁₅
          have h₁₇ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ∈ I i := by
            simpa [Int.ModEq, emod_eq_emod_iff_emod_sub_eq_zero] using h₁₆
          -- Use the fact that the difference is in every ideal to conclude it is in their intersection
          have h₁₈ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ∈ I i := h₁₇
          have h₁₉ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ∈ ⨅ i : ι, I i := h₈
          -- Use the fact that the difference is in the intersection to conclude it is zero
          have h₂₀ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val = 0 := by
            -- Use the fact that the difference is in the intersection to conclude it is zero
            have h₂₁ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ∈ ⨅ i : ι, I i := h₈
            -- Use the fact that the difference is in the intersection to conclude it is zero
            have h₂₂ : (x : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val - (y : R ⧸ ⨅ i : ι, I i).val ∈ I i := h₁₇
            -- Use the fact that the difference is in the intersection to conclude it is zero
            simp_all [Quotient.eq]
          simp_all [Quotient.eq]
        simp_all [Quotient.eq]
      simp_all [Quotient.eq]
    simp_all [Quotient.eq]
  simp_all [Quotient.eq]

refine' fun x y h => _
  have h₁ : ∀ (i : ι), (chineseMap I x) i = (chineseMap I y) i := by
    intro i
    have h₂ := congr_fun h i
    simp_all
  have h₂ : ∃ (r : R), x = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r := by
    have h₃ : x ∈ (Set.range (fun (r : R) : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i => Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r)) := by
      apply (Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective _).surjective
      <;> use x
    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := h₃
    refine' ⟨r, _⟩
    simpa [hr] using hr
  have h₃ : ∃ (s : R), y = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) s := by
    have h₄ : y ∈ (Set.range (fun (r : R) : R ⧸ ⨅ i, I i => Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r)) := by
      apply (Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective _).surjective
      <;> use y
    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h₄
    refine' ⟨s, _⟩
    simpa [hs] using hs
  obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := h₂
  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h₃
  have h₄ : ∀ (i : ι), (chineseMap I (Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r)) i = (chineseMap I (Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) s)) i := by
    intro i
    have h₅ := h₁ i
    simp_all [chineseMap_mk]
  have h₅ : ∀ (i : ι), (Quotient.mk (I i) r) = (Quotient.mk (I i) s) := by
    intro i
    have h₆ := h₄ i
    simp [chineseMap_mk] at h₆
    <;> simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
  have h₆ : r - s ∈ ⨅ i, I i := by
    have h₇ : ∀ i, r - s ∈ I i := by
      intro i
      have h₈ : (Quotient.mk (I i) r) = (Quotient.mk (I i) s) := h₅ i
      have h₉ : (Quotient.mk (I i) (r - s)) = 0 := by
        calc
          (Quotient.mk (I i) (r - s)) = (Quotient.mk (I i) r) - (Quotient.mk (I i) s) := by
            simp [Quotient.map_sub]
          _ = 0 := by rw [h₈]; simp
      have h₁₀ : (r - s) ∈ I i := by
        simpa [Quotient.eq_zero_iff] using h₉
      exact h₁₀
    exact fun i => h₇ i
  have h₇ : Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) s := by
    have h₈ : r - s ∈ ⨅ i, I i := h₆
    have h₉ : Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) (r - s) = 0 := by
      simpa [Quotient.eq_zero_iff] using h₈
    calc
      Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) r = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) (s + (r - s)) := by ring_nf
      _ = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) s + Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) (r - s) := by simp [Quotient.map_add]
      _ = Quotient.mk (⨅ i, I i) s := by simp [h₉]
  rw [hr, hs, h₇]

have h : Function.Injective (⇑(@Ideal.chineseMap R inst✝ ι I)) := by
    intro x₁ x₂ h_eq
    have h₁ : x₁ = x₂ := by
      classical
      have h₂ : x₁ = x₂ := by
        -- Since chineseMap is well-defined and respects the quotient ring structure,
        -- injectivity can be derived from the properties of the ideals.
        -- We will use the fact that the maps Quotient.mk (I i) are consistent
        -- and that the intersection of ideals is involved.
        -- However, an easier approach is to use the fact that
        -- all the above rings are modules over the base ring R and that R
        -- is a commutative ring, so the kernel of the map chineseMap is zero.
        -- But to avoid such heavy machinery, we can use a simpler argument:
        -- if two elements of the quotient R / ⨅ i, I i are mapped to the same element
        -- in ∏ i, R / I i, then their difference must belong to every I i, hence to their
        -- intersection, and therefore they are the same element in the quotient.
        -- In Lean, this can be translated as:
        -- 1) Use the fact that if two elements are mapped to the same element in the product,
        -- then all their components are the same in each quotient.
        -- 2) Lift this back to the R / (⨅ i, I i) quotient and conclude equality there.
